structure_proposals.why: The two prescribed rep ranges are inverted against what actually happens: PushA is labelled the heavier 8-10 range yet you pressed 150x10 on 2026-07-16, while PushB is labelled the lighter 10-12 range yet you pressed a heavier 157.5x10 on 2026-07-20 for the same reps. PushA buries the machine behind four sets of smith incline, so the chest is pre-fatigued there and fresh on PushB. Flip the range labels so PushB is the 8-10 heavy exposure and PushA the 10-12 exposure, and they will finally describe the sessions you are really doing.

The same lift is two different athletes across the split: 54x10 for three sets on PushB (2026-07-20) versus only 47.5x13 for two sets on PushA (2026-07-16). On PushA it sits sixth in the order, after both presses, the crossover, the shoulder press and the lateral raise, so it gets whatever is left. Move overhead-cable-triceps ahead of the lateral-raise on PushA so its one PushA exposure starts with fresh arms instead of the dregs of the session, and the whole trailing triceps block stops evaporating.
